sn13.8:1.2: “Suppose, monks, that the great ocean were to go to extinction, to its total end, except for two or three drops of water.
sn13.8:1.4: Which would be greater: the water in the great ocean that had gone to extinction, to its total end, or the two or three remaining drops of water?”
sn13.8:2.1: “Lord, the water in the great ocean that had gone to extinction, to its total end, would be far greater.
sn13.8:2.2: The two or three remaining drops of water would be next to nothing.
sn13.8:2.3: They wouldn’t be a hundredth, a thousandth, a one hundred-thousandth—the two or three remaining drops of water—when compared with the water in the great ocean that had gone to extinction, to its total end.”
sn13.8:2.4: “In the same way, monks, for a disciple of the noble ones who is consummate in view, an individual who has broken through (to stream-entry), the suffering & stress totally ended & extinguished is far greater. That which remains in the state of having at most seven remaining lifetimes is next to nothing: It’s not a hundredth, a thousandth, a one hundred-thousandth, when compared with the previous mass of suffering. That’s how great the benefit is of breaking through to the Dhamma, monks. That’s how great the benefit
sn13.8:2.5: is of obtaining the Dhamma eye.”
